level_signals: 6+ years of product management or related industry roles
Represents topline safety metric to senior leadership
Owns strategy for suite of safety measurement platforms company-wide

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: Have 6+ years of product management or related industry roles, with specific expertise in AI safety, trust & safety, integrity, or related domains.
must_haves.qualifier: with specific expertise in AI safety, trust & safety, integrity, or related domains

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: Have a background in data science, statistics, economics, or related fields.
Are deeply curious and interested in interdisciplinary fields such as human-computer interaction, psychology, philosophy, or similar areas.
Have hands-on experience driving consensus and action in ambiguous spaces.
Excel at identifying and challenging underlying assumptions and constraints through insightful questioning.
Are highly effective at cross-functional collaboration and communicating complex technical concepts clearly and persuasively.
Enjoy working in a fast-paced, high-growth environment.

scope.surface: Safety measurement platforms and products used across OpenAI; topline safety metrics for frontier models
primary_metrics_inferred: Topline safety metric reported to senior leadership
core_responsibilities: Partner closely with data science, research, engineering, policy teams, and other stakeholders to craft a vision for understanding safety outcomes and prevalence on our platforms.
Define strategic priorities and product roadmaps focused on improving safety measurement approaches will scaling our measurement platform to more use cases, products, and cross-functional team needs.
Establish repeatable processes to integrate cutting-edge AI safety research into OpenAI's safety measurement products.
Develop and continuously refine clear, actionable success criteria that effectively capture our ambitions with safety measurement.
Represent quantitative progress on safety to senior leadership.

green_flags: Clear ownership of a company-wide strategic function (topline safety metric)
Direct interface with senior leadership — high visibility role
Equal opportunity employer with explicit accommodation policy
Mission-driven work with tangible societal impact
red_flags: All requirements are framed under 'You might thrive in this role if you' — ambiguous whether these are hard requirements or preferred qualifications
Role is onsite San Francisco only; no remote option mentioned

ambiguities: All requirements are listed under 'You might thrive in this role if you' which traditionally implies preferences, not hard requirements. However, the 6+ years PM experience with specific domain expertise, data science background, and cross-functional capabilities read as de facto hard requirements given the seniority and specificity. Classified as hard accordingly.
No explicit hybrid or remote policy stated beyond 'based in San Francisco, CA with relocation assistance available' — treated as onsite but a hybrid arrangement cannot be ruled out.
